title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Python basics!

questions.question: What is Python primarily used for?
questions.explanation: Python is a general-purpose programming language used for web development, AI, data science, automation, and much more!

questions.question: Which function displays output to the screen?
questions.explanation: The print() function is used to display output to the screen in Python.

questions.question: Python is case-sensitive, meaning 'Print' and 'print' are different.
questions.explanation: Python is case-sensitive! 'Print', 'print', and 'PRINT' are all treated as different things.

questions.question: What symbol starts a comment in Python?
questions.explanation: In Python, the # symbol is used to start a comment. Everything after # on that line is ignored.

questions.question: Text in Python must be wrapped in _____ or double quotes.
questions.explanation: Strings (text) in Python must be enclosed in either single quotes ('text') or double quotes ("text").

questions.question: What is the output of: print(5 + 3)?
questions.explanation: Python calculates 5 + 3 = 8 first, then prints the result.

questions.question: Python requires semicolons at the end of each line.
questions.explanation: Python does NOT require semicolons at the end of lines. Just press Enter to start a new statement.

questions.question: How many spaces is the standard Python indentation?
questions.explanation: The Python style guide (PEP 8) recommends 4 spaces for each level of indentation.

questions.question: Which is a valid variable name in Python?
questions.explanation: Variable names can't start with numbers, contain hyphens, or have spaces. my_value is valid!

questions.question: The escape character _____ creates a new line in Python strings.
questions.explanation: The \n escape character creates a new line (like pressing Enter) inside a string.

questions.question: What does print("Hello", "World") output?
questions.explanation: When using commas in print(), Python adds a space between items, resulting in 'Hello World'.

questions.question: Comments in Python are executed by the interpreter.
questions.explanation: Comments are completely ignored by Python. They are only for humans to read!
